description_long: This harp features a lyre-shaped frame with two gracefully curved wooden arms extending upward and outward from a rounded, hollow body. The front of the body is lighter in color and has a flat soundboard with six evenly spaced strings running vertically to a simple tuning bar at the top. The back of the body displays a darker, textured wood grain, giving it a natural, handcrafted appearance. The side views reveal the harp's slim profile and the gentle arch of the arms, as well as the rounded, bulbous back of the resonating chamber. The instrument appears to be constructed primarily from wood, with metal or nylon strings. Its design is minimalistic, with no visible decorative carvings or embellishments.
description: A lyre-shaped wooden harp with six strings, featuring a rounded body and gracefully curved arms. The front is light wood with a flat soundboard, while the back is darker and textured.
description_view_0: Front view showing the light-colored soundboard, six vertical strings, and the open, lyre-shaped frame.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slim profile, gentle arch of the arms, and the rounded, bulbous back of the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the darker, textured wood grain and the full curvature of the frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the harp's thin profile and the rounded back.
attribute_frame_design: The frame is lyre-shaped, with two smoothly curved wooden arms arching outward from a rounded, hollow body and connecting to a simple crossbar at the top.
attribute_column_curvature: The column is highly arched, forming a wide, symmetrical curve on both sides.
